source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/01_DHF/05_HnX-P1, HnX-PB/03_설계개발 출력/10_User manual/[HnX-PB] User Manual(EU) (A-IFU-HNX(B)-EU, Rev 0.5, 260429).docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: User manual for the HnX-PB battery-powered portable X-ray system, including intended use, safety warnings, regulatory standards, technical specifications, operation, maintenance, and service information.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use as manufacturer instructions for safe operation, radiation protection, maintenance, calibration, and regulatory reference for the HnX-PB Portable X-ray System.
keyPoints: The HnX-PB is a battery-powered portable X-ray system intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.
The device is for professional healthcare facilities and is to be operated only by qualified healthcare professionals or trained service personnel.
The manual lists medical electrical safety, EMC, radio, FDA, FCC, and radiation-related standards and guidance documents.
The HnX-PB is classified as Class I or internally powered ME equipment, IPX0, continuous operation, and not suitable around flammable anesthetic mixtures or oxygen-rich environments.
The device emits ionizing radiation and requires facility access controls, operator training, protective clothing, minimum distance, and usage logging.
Software/Firmware is identified as HnX_PB Version 00.13.

concepts.name: Intended purpose
concepts.evidence: The HnX-PB is a battery-powered portable X-ray system intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.

concepts.name: Indications for use
concepts.evidence: The device is indicated for the diagnostic imaging of the following clinical conditions: Traumatic and Orthopedic Conditions; Respiratory and Thoracic Conditions; Abdominal Conditions.

concepts.name: Contra-indications
concepts.evidence: Pregnancy: The device is contraindicated for use in the abdominal and pelvic regions of women who are pregnant or may be pregnant.

concepts.name: Pediatric population warning
concepts.evidence: The device is not intended for use in the Pediatric population, which includes Children (patients aged 2 years and above) and Infants (patients under 2 years old).

concepts.name: Radiation safety
concepts.evidence: When X-ray is being irradiated, the operator shall be stand at least 1.8meters(6feet) from the patient, the X-ray tube, and out of the useful beam.

concepts.name: Restricted use and access control
concepts.evidence: This device is intended for use only by qualified healthcare professionals or trained service personnel.

concepts.name: EMC safety
concepts.evidence: HnX-PB requires special precautions regarding EMC and needs to be operated only by authorized personnel and put into service according to EMC information provided in the user manual.

concepts.name: Medical equipment classification
concepts.evidence: Type of protection against electrical shock Class I or Internally powered ME equipment; Degree of protection against ingress of water IPX0; Mode of operation Continuous operation.

concepts.name: Bluetooth Low Energy thumbnail preview
concepts.evidence: A thumbnail image is transmitted from the PC to the Control Unit using Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) wireless communication (module: BoT-nLE521, frequency: 2,402–2,480 MHz).

concepts.name: Regular inspection and maintenance
concepts.evidence: In order to ensure the safety of patients and operator, to maintain the performance and reliability of the device, be sure to perform regular inspection at least once a year.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is a portable X-ray system.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is a portable X-ray System.

evidence.claim: The user manual only pertains to the HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: This User Manual only pertains to the HnX-PB and does not serve for any other products of the company.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is intended to generate radiographic images of human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is a battery-powered portable X-ray system intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is intended for professional healthcare facilities and controlled indoor environments.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is intended for use in professional healthcare facilities, such as hospitals, clinics, and emergency departments. The device is limited to indoor operation within controlled environments where radiation safety protocols and distance requirements are maintained.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is not intended for pediatric use.
evidence.evidenceText: The device is not intended for use in the Pediatric population, which includes Children (patients aged 2 years and above) and Infants (patients under 2 years old).

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is contraindicated for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Mammography: This device is not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB emits ionizing radiation during normal use.
evidence.evidenceText: This device emits ionizing radiation during normal use.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is not intended for fluoroscopic use.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is designed for radiographic uses only and not intended for fluoroscopic use or other associated applications.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB should be used for diagnosis and not therapy.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB should be used for the diagnosis purpose and is not intended for therapy of any kind.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is Class I and IPX0 under EN 60601-1 standards.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is specified as Class I and IPX0 equipment under EN 60601-1 standards.

evidence.claim: Regulatory classification includes Class I or internally powered ME equipment, IPX0, and continuous operation.
evidence.evidenceText: Type of protection against electrical shock Class I or Internally powered ME equipment; Degree of protection against ingress of water IPX0; Mode of operation Continuous operation.

evidence.claim: The product safety standards include IEC 60601-1, IEC 60601-1-2, IEC 60601-1-3, IEC 60601-2-28, IEC 60601-2-54, IEC 62304, IEC 62366-1, IEC 60601-1-6, EN ISO 15223-1, radio standards, and EN 62311.
The device conforms to 21 CFR 1020 Subchapter J and 21 CFR 1020.30.
evidence.evidenceText: The device also conforms to the following: • 21 CFR 1020 Subchapter J: Performance Standards for Ionizing Radiation Emitting Products • 21 CFR 1020.30: Diagnostic x-ray system and their major components

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB conforms to IEC60601-1-2:2020 for immunity and emissions.
evidence.evidenceText: HnX-PB conforms to this IEC60601-1-2:2020 standard for both immunity and emissions.

evidence.claim: The device requires facility-implemented access controls because it lacks built-in user authentication.
evidence.evidenceText: This device emits ionizing radiation and lacks built-in user authentication. Unauthorized operation may result in inappropriate radiation exposure to patients, operators, or bystanders.

evidence.claim: Only qualified healthcare professionals or trained service personnel should use the device.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is intended for use only by qualified healthcare professionals or trained service personnel.

evidence.claim: Operators should stand at least 1.8 meters from the patient, X-ray tube, and useful beam during irradiation.
evidence.evidenceText: When X-ray is being irradiated, the operator shall be stand at least 1.8meters(6feet) from the patient, the X-ray tube, and out of the useful beam.

evidence.claim: During exposure, operators should wear protective clothing and stand behind lead shielding.
evidence.evidenceText: During X-ray exposure, the operator should wear a qualified protective clothing e.g. apron of min. 0.5 mm lead equivalence or 1.6mm(1/16inch) lead equivalence and stand behind the lead screen or the lead glass screen.

evidence.claim: The maximum output of the X-ray generator is 720 W.
evidence.evidenceText: The maximum output of X-ray generator is 720W

evidence.claim: The X-ray tube output voltage range is 50 to 90 kV.
evidence.evidenceText: The X-ray Tube output voltage of generator is 50~90kV

evidence.claim: The software/firmware is HnX_PB Version 00.13.
evidence.evidenceText: Software(Firmware): HnX_PB Version: 00.13

evidence.claim: The Bluetooth module is BoT-nLE521 using Bluetooth Low Energy in the 2,402 to 2,480 MHz frequency range.
evidence.evidenceText: Bluetooth (BLE) Module BoT-nLE521 Standard Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) Frequency Range 2,402 – 2,480 MHz

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd. in the Republic of Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer: H&abyz Co., Ltd. 2-dong, 41-16, Cheoinseong-ro, Namsa-eup, Cheoin-gu, Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, 17118, Republic of Korea

evidence.claim: The European Authorized Representative is Javitech Consulting GmbH.
evidence.evidenceText: European Authorized Representative Javitech Consulting GmbH Sachsenhausener Str. 16, 65824 Schwalbach a. Ts. Germany

evidence.claim: The manufacturing country is Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturing country: Made in Korea

evidence.claim: The estimated product lifetime is up to 7 years with appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.
evidence.evidenceText: The estimated product lifetime is up to 7 years under appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.

reviewFlags.reason: Document revision and document date are taken from the source file path, not from a clearly labeled document-control section in the body text.

Authority or jurisdiction is ambiguous because the source path indicates EU and the text includes European Authorized Representative and CE Marking, but the regulatory section also cites FDA guidance, FCC rules, U.S. 510(k), and 21 CFR requirements.
The source appears to be preprocessed from a DOCX and some figure references, symbols, table formatting, and icon placeholders are missing or degraded.
The manual contains both operation manual and service information, including calibration and repair instructions.
